channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Germany Is Primed For Economic Boom — 33 replies — 132.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Germany Is Primed For Economic Boom

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-10 11:50 UTC

OP

Germany is in the middle of the biggest economic shift since the invention of the steam engine and it is over the halfway mark: Energiewende.

Now, with clean efficient renewable energy Germany has began explosive investment into AI and modern tech industries. The largest economy in Europe and the third largest in the world has entered the ring against the US tech giants. With modern energy and while the rest of the world struggles with oil shortages and the Iranian crisis (and the Trump crisis), the timing could not be better.

There is often negativity surrounding Germany, both here and broadly in the media. However, these negative sentiments are often sensationalist and poorly considered. This video explains well why Germany is still in a fantastic economic position. He does not mention it, but the increased energy independence with cheap clean renewable sources is even more beneficial for manufacturing. The growing pains are over and now the goliath can awaken.
https://youtu.be/cg43s4PkPX0

This new green economy also creates a host of growth opportunities in renewables. Both domestically but also abroad when other countries are forced to go through the process Germany has in order to modernize their energy. Fossil fuels must end we cannot continue to rely upon them or the consequences will be disaster. Hopefully, Americans can be convinced of this before they doom us all.

Which also brings us to the last issue: PPP. Based on OECD data, equivalent prices in Germany would be 81% of what they are in America. Even this data fails to capture the entire story in my opinion because it is all prices. The reality is necessary goods like healthcare, food, and education are far cheaper in Germany while consumer goods are more similar. The result not only are Germans with the same income relatively richer in all areas but the economy is more equal as only luxury goods are truly expensive because there are not allowed these corporate monopolies on the basic needs of life.

Early replies (first 3)

62338713
>>62338691
Kek nice fanfiction

62338716
>>62338691
Europeans are too lazy, overregulated, and overtaxed to grow

62338787
Germany will never be allowed to boom, as punishment for WWII. Their resources will be extracted, but they won't see a red cent.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339978
>>62338716
Americans are lazy and fat but they steal wealth with their army.

62339986
>>62338691
bait used to be believable

62340965
Late stage soviet propaganda be like >>62338691

62341014
Yeah na, the biggest boom for germany would be the rest of europe beginning to pay their own bills.

62341040
Isn't Merz one of the lowest rated european leaders right now and the AfD hovering around 30% in the polls?
If Germany is doing so well why aren't people voting for the status quo?

62341937
The electric jew wants us to deny our own eyes and experiences we live everyday. I rebuke you in the name of Jesus.

62342033
>>62341040
>If Germany is doing so well why aren't people voting for the status quo
Russian disinformation spreading on YouTube making people miserable. Germany just needs to ban the internet at this point for the sake of the country.

62342055
>>62341040
Afd will tank to 10% when they finally get into the government and fail because they aren't professional same as all the other far right leaders.
They all have brain damage so they're not competent enough to enact their policies without crashing their own reputation.

62342081
>>62341040
They are not doing well. Imagine if a country is run by Democrats for several decades. That's the case because Merkel also "ruled" like a Democrat. They never got their shit pushed in like in normal countries. And now Germany is ruled by one of the dumbest, least impressive political classes all over the world

62342102
No

Also. Note that in China and Russia. Industrial subsidises residential. In Europe and America. The people subsidise the oligarchs and monopolies.

62342116
>>62338691

62342159
Fuck off, you fan fiction writing kraut muncher.

62343395
>>62338691
>new train station beeing build in stuttgart
>they started in 2010
>new prediction is 2031
>costs absurdly high
>almost all projects where the state is responsible take absurdly long and are absurdly expensive
yeah gg and no re, we cant even replace our infrastucture anymore at this rate

62343435
>>62343395
>cologne wanted to restaurate this abdomination of a opera building
>started 2012, plan was finished by 2015 and 250 million cost
>STILL building
>cost now 1.5 billion
if you would make comedy out of it it wouldnt be funny since its too absurd
if german burocrazy is responsible for something you cant overestimate how hard they will fail and yes absurd regulations are the top reason

62343442
>>62343395
>declining population
>can't build things
>50%+ taxes on all working people

But the west will totally grow lol

62344827
>11B investment into AI
Meanhwile a fucking vscode fork is worth triple that

62344942
>>62338691
I think Europe, and in particular "old" Europe, will be one of the big winners of AI.
It just seems like a really low hanging fruit for AI to do compliance shit and other regulatory tasks. If you eliminate or greatly reduce the cost of the compliance, you see a solid boost in productivity/earnings.

62344950
>>62339978

>relaxed
>well fed
>superior power

truly genius

62344958
>>62338787

This requires Germans to play along with the corrupt system though that oppresses them.

62344961
>>62338691
By boom you mean it will explode into 1000 pieces?

62345010
>>62338691
As a German. Some of the thing you say are true BUT company and investment only care about 2 things the cost of energy and the cost of labour and both are through the roof. You dont attract new capital and companies operating in Germany are leaving.

62345039
>>62338691
Honestly yeah. It's not as bad as mutts here love to say. I work as ee technician for a renewables company and we've never had more work. Attached fields are growing too. Structural reorganization is needed and under way. Legacy industries will be hit hard. But unemployment is pretty stable given that, especially in CE, there's a lot of free positions. Finally something is happening in EU.

62345066
>>62344942
But they will protect women's fake email girl jobs at all costs.
You really think they'll let white people reproduce?
No, the main purpose of email girl jobs is to prevent white babies from being born.
AI cannot touch that.

62345122
It’s funny how americans at the same time can’t stop seething about the k-shaped economy but also think that everything socialism-related destroys the country. Truly a sad people.

62345142
>>62345122
When I was at university I thought I was going to move to USA to rake in the big money. Instead I ended up taking a job for shit salary (relatively speaking, I still have all I need) in Norway and instead I'm raking in that quality of life. I have so much free time, I have a job that doesn't stress me out, I can literally walk to work and I get to spend so much time outdoors. I don't really need expensive habits when I can live like this anyways.

This made me realize what is really wrong with USA. The reason they outperform us yuropoors on economy is that they have made it incredibly shit to be poor as a way to whip people into working 80 hour weeks. This would be insanity here, but there it's still preferable to being poor.

62345151
As a German that would be great news but I don't see it. Who's supposed to work in this new green high tech economy? Indians?

62345198
>>62338691
wtf are you talking about?

62345283
>>62345122

Socialism creates the K shaped economy you absolute moron

62345297
>>62345198
That's wholesale price btw

62345329
>>62338691
Ai-2027.com

Germany is playing catchup and is not making any recovery.

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 33 replies — 132.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-10 11:44 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339395
>>62339382
Why monero?

62339401
>>62339328
>Anyone buying ICP?

62339417
>>62339395
why monero? people actually use it, the fact that bitcoin is transparent is actually quite a con in itself. stablecoins, ETH and its L2s (think polygon, defi, smart contracts etc) and privacy coins have genuine utility; something like HYPE has utility.

Anything with bitcoin it's just about its "heckin price" that's all it is, nobody uses it.

62339452
>>62339417
>Nobody uses Bitcoin
Bro… The largest world conflict at this very moment involves Bitcoin.

62339462
>>62339452
>2 months ago

62339510
>>62339328
I don't feel bad, I sold near 100k, 120k and 100k again to buy this dip in stocks and especially gold.
I will be rebuying BTC lower starting August.

62339716
>>62339349
Thanks for your sacrifice. The bull market appreciates your contribution.

62339730
>>62339328

No regrets here. I did have over 2BTC back in 2013-2014 and if i had just decided to hold, it would've all been gone now because the exchange they were on (BTC-E) got shut down by the DOJ and unlike with Mt.Gox, users pretty much got shafted.

You can't just pick and choose ideal past moments from life to do differently, that's not how shit works. If you had that kind of clairvoyance you could easily just win the lottery or just time the current market perfectly as if you're insider-trading to become rich within a matter of weeks.

62339878
>>62339372
Why did you regret buying?

62339883
>>62339328
Sold it with no regrets, tripled my initial investment, moved it into stocks which actually move up
Some posters laugh at boomer stocks but btc and most crypto has been going down for well over a year, even shit boomer stocks that barely beat inflation are better, at this point crypto is dead and the days of it doing massive 10x in a day will never happen again

62340066
>>62339328
>Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?
sold 0.3 above 90k, still have 1.7 left.
i hve 31k to buy back in october.

i also sold 0.6 below 40k and i do regret that.

62340438
>>62339382
What's your price goal for xmr?

62341003
>>62339395
privacy maybe

62341032
>>62339417
you've been conned, the fact that you think eth have any utility is a clear sign of that

62341036
>>62339328
Sold at the top. No regrets. will buy back in after saylor dumps his load

62342205
>>62339401
And why would you buy this?

62342982
>>62339417
I don't think it's fair to say nobody uses Bitcoin. It's just that Bitcoin's dominant use case ended up being different from what many people originally expected.

62344142
>>62339452
They're just blind about it

62344890
Is it good to buy now?

62344894
>>62339328
if you didn't sell it when it was over 100k it was not the right move

62345001
>>62344890
Anon, if you believe in BTC long term, buying is the easy part. Holding is harder. And now there are even ways to put BTC to work through native staking without giving up self-custody. Do with that information what you will.

62345004
>>62339462
What's the problem with that?

62345048
>>62344894
You can buy the dip ser

62345050
>>62341036
Buy eth and sol too

62345054
>>62340066
What's happening in October?

62345074
>>62345048
yea but you could have bought the 50% discount with some of the profits instead of fresh shekels

62345096
>>62345074
Smart guy

62345100
>>62345001
How can we stake btc?

62345127
hold till $1M

62345299
>>62345054
were supposed to bottom, 4 year cycle or some shit.

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 23 replies — 83.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-10 11:38 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

62339778
Imagine caring this much, holy shit. Adult bully victim no doubt

62339781
>>62339778
Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.

62339829
>>62339778
idk bout that... xrp apparently lives rent free in linkbaggies minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339835
>>62339829
100%
And I caught him red handed

And Chainlinkies be all like: "Durrr imagine caring this isn't important"

62340175
>>62339844
Xrp hasn't reclaimed it's all time high after a whole decade. If xrp baggies didn't sell they have blown their chance of limiting their losses and buying something useful

Kaboom

62340182
>>62339768
Its biepum the yeet node operator
Give him a break, chainlink shilling and xrp fudding is his personality

62340345
>>62339768
so fxcking bullish

62340350
Boooomers and peelers won

62340365
>>62339768
>Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62340374
>>62339768
Why would anyone buy Chainlink or xrp I don't understand. Crypto is there to buy drugs online. Can you buy drugs online with Chainlink ? No. Can you buy drugs online with xrp? No. Case closed.

62340393
>>62339768
I don't hold link either tho
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62340516
everybody hates u dumb stinks

62340978
>>62339781
>words words words
It's funny how you're immediately identifiable by the spergy seethe that radiates from your posts.

Even funnier, >>62339844 posting the madmen meme.
>"We don't think about Linkies at all!!"
>Op literally creates a thread ranting about Chainlink because some other random thread that doesn't mention Chainlink could have been made by a Linkie

62340989
>>62340978
>TLDR linkjeet seethe

didnt read lol

62340992
BOOOOOOOOOOM

BULLISH AT XRP

62341232
Bumping this bullsih thread
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62342495
>>62339768
>My retarded shitposts made someone so mad he made an expose thread on me
FXCKING BULLISH KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62343674
>>62339768
>>62339781
>>62339835
>>62340989
>linkcucks and xrpaypigs having a retarded biz slapfight as they both go to 0

62344317
KABOOOOOOOOM>>62339768

62344795
>>62339781
This. I don't hold either but the link spam is so fucking stupid and annoying. They got decimated in a war length campaign of threads and saw zero wins and now it is over and over with this same shitty failed meme that ironically got funny when XRP posters just did it back to them to mock them. I have zero dog in this race but XRP is the clear winner in the messaging war.

62344908
>>62344795
>t. the same person a different id
manufacturing consensus on a dead board is pretty cringe desu senpai

62345267
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 186 replies — 64.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 186 • Updated: 2026-06-10 09:02 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336610
>>62332425
>You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.
Still here. Feel free to also refute my point on your BTC comparison falling apart due to the flow of change of hands from new BTC to node operators to market versus new Link tokens going from CLL's uncirculated supply directly to Binance instead of node operators.

62336627
>>62335458
p+c bitch

62336936
>>62316605
>Chainlink pays the nodes from their own team wallets

>>62336610
>Link tokens don't go from CLL's uncirculated supply to node operators

You're a fucking retard you know that.

62337435
>>62333330
Epstein should have stored his blackmail material in here amirite

62337475
If chainlink is decentralized why do not even the biggest, stinkiest linkies here brag about running a node?

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

62344923
>>62342602
All of Chainlink's tokens go through Binance, including node payments.
Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

item.title: >$3-4k isn't enough to live on in burgerland >wtf VA disability is $3-4K a month, i'd never have to work a day in my lif… — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>$3-4k isn't enough to live on in burgerland >wtf VA disability is $3-4K a month, i'd never have to work a day in my lif…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-10 11:49 UTC

OP

>$3-4k isn't enough to live on in burgerland
>wtf VA disability is $3-4K a month, i'd never have to work a day in my life if I got that it's so unfair!
so which is it /biz/? can you or can you not live on $3-4K a month in the US?

Early replies (first 3)

62342119
you can
if you own your home and it's not a gigantic mansion in vhcol area you can even live off social security

62342132
you can remain alive but you cannot truly live

62342138
>>62342112
You can definitely live off of that. 30 years of inflation later, you probably won't be able to. And if you're just getting $3k/mo it's going to be hard to invest enough to keep up.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62342213
>>62342138
will I still be here in 30 years tho?
>>62342132
are any of us truly living as wagecucks anyway?

62342240
To get VA bennies you have to be a zogbot, no thanks.

62342251
>>62342112
Of course you can live on $4k a month, mind you VA disability is TAX FREE, so whatever number you are talking about, 3 or 4k is NET income.

You can easily get an apartment for $1k or less in the vast majority of the USA, yeah you're not gonna live in Honolulu, LA or NYC but you could easily afford many decent cities.

If you're willing to spend $1500 a month in rent you can live in almost any city , with very few being unobtainable. $4k a month is plenty to live a good stress free life.

Assume rent is $1500, you live in a good area, you have $2500 left over. Assume bills (electric, internet, cell phone, food) is $750 a month. you now have $1750 left to spend on whatever you want, maybe you lease a brand new car for $300 a month. Now you have $1450 a left a month, plenty for a reasonable life, you can go out to eat, see movies, live a life of leisure.

If you cant make it on $4k net per month you're just dumb as fuck honestly. That shit is a golden ticket.

62342271
>>62342251
what about $3300 a month before taxes, passive income and no rent? that's how much a friend I know makes (although he won't tell me what his passive income is from) and he tells me he can't retire like that.

62342273
>>62342251
you're supposed to pay off your house after your first deployment and then medically retire
imagine retiring when you dont have a place to live

62342290
>>62342273
You can easily get a VA loan on VA disability. They know its guaranteed income so the approval is easy. Would be trivial to buy a nice $200k home somewhere affordable like Columbia, SC. Good infrastructure and mix of affordability.

62345226
>>62342119
I own my house and live in TN. The shittest medical insurance with a $15k deductible is $2,000 a month. Food for me and my kids is $1500 a month buying fresh vegetables and meats from Walmart. Buy somehwre else can dbl the price.

Pets get sick? 2 grand bill from vet. Kids need clothes? more money.
car has a proble,... another couple grand.

it just nmever ends. if i own a house and am underwater i cannot imagine the struggle for wagies renting

62345253
>>62342112
USA is a big country with a lot of variability of cost of living. i live in the suburbs of philly. my monthly bills are $3.2k all-in (rent, utilities, food, car insurance, phone, etc.) if i wanted to live near blacks and puerto ricans i could lower this to $3k/mo. if i wanted roommates it might be $2.5k/mo. if i lived on rice and beans it might be $2.3k/mo. if i moved to a rural shithole with no jobs it might be $1.7k/mo. you get the idea.

62345326
>>62345226
does your state offer medicaid or is it another righttarded shithole that defunded it? you might consider gibsmaxxing.

62337787

62338026

item.title: Housing market — 9 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Housing market

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-10 10:57 UTC

OP

I kept hearing about a crash last year so I wanted to get out of a fixer upper and buy something in better condition later instead of paying for HVAC and structural stuff. What's the forecast looking like now?

Early replies (first 3)

62344130
probably going to crab dump for the next decade

62344306
>>62344043
I've been putting off buying a house for almost four years now because of an allegedly imminent housing market crash that everyone keeps assuring me is just around the corner.

62344344
>>62344306

Yeah but in those for years to be fair we did see a drop from the top of the pandemic shit. This time it's about macro factors and the Hormussy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62344351
>>62344344
I'll wait 2 more weeks.

62344371
Nothing means anything and it is unlikely to change.

62344892
>>62344043
lmao at your image

62344940
The housing market always crashes a month after you buy.

62345088
>>62344043
You sound retarded so it was always over for you.

62345157
>>62344043
Obviously you're a retard and there was no crash. People say bullshit all the time. If you hear it and believe it, then you obviously lack the willpower to check, and it's not hard.

Housing market is actually pretty stable right now. Average price of a home is now 500k from 400k. If you can buy or build a home or a rental, now is the time before it's 600k. That might seem expensive to you, but guess what, the economy doesn't give a shit about sticker shock.

Or you can start doing what I think people should've been doing a long time ago, and build low cost housing. Yah know, like they did in the 40s-70s and even prior, 1 story houses that house 5 people comfortably for like 200k value in today's money. It's probably worth 300k+ cause it doesn't exist on the market, but it is worth less than the 500k houses, so you probably have a lot more demand from being able to sell to poor people, thus it's less risky an investment. Just make sure you sell it for at least 100k profit.

item.title: After all this time i finally know what they meant — 4 replies — 5.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
After all this time i finally know what they meant

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-10 11:13 UTC

OP

Remember cicada 3301 and everything around them?

The whole thing about getting information for free and privacy finally happens and it makes sense, finally connecting the dots.

Were slowly getting cutted off free access to information, mostly by AI. I think and thus i am predicting that information in near future will be only accessible via AI, and it wont be top tier quality bc of data poisoning.

And the privacy part, for this day theres no way to "go dark". Like, literally, u can't: tor is traceable, every packet flows thru america's CIA tap datacenters, there're satellites everywhere with high resolution and different types cameras and lenses, ur phone can be traced even when its off; someone can call u and within 50 seconds can reconnect to ur phone and listen what ur talking (u can do it as a civil lol).


I am scared of the future.

Early replies (first 3)

62344719
Do you like our panopticon?
https://youtu.be/Fzhkwyoe5vI?si=MUw2lxHNq8tAkVxl

62344786
>someone can call u and within 50 seconds can reconnect to ur phone and listen what ur talking (
source: some pajeet attention grabbing newsletter

62345150
>>62344719
>ironic si link

>>62344786
I did security for cell phones in some shady industry a few years ago. I won't talk about the findings due to NDA, but I can say there are a few surprises. But that doesn't really matter, the problem is the profiling at scale. The only way for a non-sponsored revolution to happen now is an accidental sudden breakdown of living conditions for a big part of the "population" that can clearly be attributed to the system. As long as there are first movers, they can isolate and punish them. AI taking the negotiation power of most people means society is done, we are on the way out. Funny thing is that with superhuman AI, ownership is just a piece of paper. They will find out, not by some Skynet event, but a systemic loss of power. In just one or two years, no one will be able to competitively invest without AI getting effectively full control over the assets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345187
Go to fucking x with this shit or TikTok, actually I have a better idea start a podcast! I bet women really dig your qanon stories at parties. What’s your sign babe?

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 104 replies — 25.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-06-10 07:48 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62328962
>>62309503
are you dumb or something mate what the fuck the thing went to 58k already

62329036
>>62328962

THIS

op confirmed tard

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!

62333686
>>62309503
I bought the rally (my first cycle) and the dips/bottom. waiting on the good days now

62333871
>>62309503

62334072
63K REJECTED

62334365
>>62309503
>65K is the bottom
But it went lower anon?

62334381
>>62329970
you can buy anything you want with a coinbase card, what're you on about geez?

62336135
THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING AT ALL OF THE BEAR SHILLS

YOU LOST. YOU SOLD THE BOTTOM

THE BUYROG WINS AGAIN

BEARSBANE

62336205
>>62309503

62336221
>>62336205
It is never going 55k. I have no idea why PM metal shills (most of the bear is PM fags, we tested it) watch silver get ended by the Fed, and decide to lash out trolling people's financial lives.

I am glad the BUYROG convinced some people to not miss yet another dip.

62336238
>>62336135

62337880

YOU LOST

62340948

62341211
>>62340948
Sorry, I can’t hear anything with the noise of all these coins crashing at the same time.

62341507
>>62341211
just bought $20k MSTY

62342143

62344662
Bobobros, it’s not really crashing anymore…

62344689
>>62309503

62344772
>>62344689

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 54 replies — 15.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-06-10 08:23 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62342939
>>62342700
>>62342744
>>62342756
>>62342807
>>62342811
>>62342888
>>62342903
>>62342905
Any life is better then to keep making the same threads on /biz/ and spamposting in it

62342948
>>62342700
City tards will be dead within 10 years from starvation and disease. Laugh it up while you can.

62342997
>>62342948
Fuck you ruralfag

62343037
>>62342700
kill yourself you stupid fucking r*ddit spacing bot.

62343068
all you fags are fags.

Blue collar work is important also. Sure wiring can be done pre-fab for new construction, but here's a fun fact: 100% of all the houses that have already been built have already been built.
That means that their cast iron pipes are about to collapse, or their pvc pipes are about to come unglued or crack. It means that if they want to redo the kitchen, someone has to come tear out the old cabinets and install new ones and install new light fixtures and move the gas line and put a heated tile floor in.

It means that the HVAC company you hire to do that has equipment that breaks, and when you shove baby wipes in the toilet and it clog, you have to hire gus to come snake that shit out.

I have a friend who's son is slightly autist and apprenticed as a welder at age 15. At 16, he got hired by a company to be the shop welder for the summer: $35/hr, 40 hrs a week. That's $70k a year, but remember, he was only 16.

Anyway, you can live a good life on $100k if:
You buy a small house and do your own repairs instead of having to hire some other dildo to do it.
Get a 6 year old Tacoma and drive it for 20 years

Once you have those costs under control, you should have lots of spare money to invest.

62343110
>>62342700
>child labor

62343166
>>62343110
Not really. Better than him not doing shit all summer. He already has to take 5 days off.

62343167
>>62342798
>da booooooots
Kek

62343175
>>62343166
child labor is worse than not doing shit all summer. At that age he should study, fuck and do drugs instead of adopting the goy mentality

62343255
>>62343175
There's absolutely zero things wrong with child labor when it involves doing normal ass jobs adults do with the same standards.

People always jump to kids being kept as slaves or beaten or made to carry explosives or work in some dangerous factory without training and it's stupid.

There's zero reason why a 12 year old kid shouldn't be alloweded to get paid to wipe down tables at a McDonald's or bus tables at Applebee's over his summer.

62343264
>>62342700
Had a "white collar" semi-richfag come to me one time for help. He was scared to death to open the radiator cap so he could add water and I told him how to check by pressing the upper hose to see whether there was any pressure or not. Come to find out he didn't even know where to add more water, he didn't know what the radiator cap was or how to open it but was adamant about how dangerous it was so he wouldn't do it. How do these people drive a car?

62343298
>>62342700
What happens when you sit on ifunny and read too many monarchtrump posts, gay ass op get off the board

62343302
>>62343264
they live comfortable lives and any discomforts can be solved with money

62343318
>>62343255
Your lack of education is showing.

62343323
Daily autistic browncel thread

62343328
>>62342700
Stop making these awful fucking threads

62343411
>>62343318
You are a fucking redditor.
>>62343328
Shut up.

62344022
>>62342700
I work a blue collar job.
No overtime
Air conditioned factory
I get a nice comfy chair
Basically watch a CNC machine all day.
Will be bumped up to $139,000 in 2027.
Feels good.

62344029
I guess im technically a blue collar? I work in a factory kinda supervising trades but I dont really do anything stuff myself. 250k tho.

62344036
The elites in power want the actual workers keeping civilization alive poor because they know what is to come.
>sit in a office all day? 300k!!
>create and fix everything? 40k and you should make less!
Always destroy anything you can when fixing these fuckers houses.

62344177
>>62344022
But that’s not even middle class.

62344183
>>62344029
Yeah, still blue collar unfortunately.

62344186
>>62344036
Not really, a fourth worlder can come into usa and do the job.

62344274
>>62342735
I think it's funny

62344383
>>62344274
My threads are funny? Wtf do you mean? What is so funny about my threads?

62344425
fuck trade school. anyone smart should learn to vibe code an app that uses crypto.

62344611
>>62342770
Those people are ether:
1. Retired
2. Trust fund leeches
Now you might say, “nuh uh, they are not parasitic blood sucking invertebrates”
And to that I would say, yes they are.
Money has to be spread out even to the non contributing members and often times instead of focusing on growing their portion; they spend energy on stealing it from the producing members.
About 1/4 of every family is productive.
About 1/2 are ambitious (not profitable)
And 4/4 are living luxuriously
That formula produces 1 producer, 2 layabouts and 1 thief; on average, in every generation.
Basically somebody has to be making the money wether it’s a company ran by the one ambitious and smart family member or my tax dollars in which case you are only advocating for leeching off of somebody and floating the idea it should be me and most people on this board.
So please fuck off and kys, immediately
Please

62344720
You know biz is in dire straits when it's gone the way of /ck/ with so many bot threads to generate a false sense of activity.... Remember to sage these threads

62344811
>>62342770
How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money? Like 10-20k might sound like " a lot of money", but even if you aren't rich thats like a year or two of savings, how is that shit going to last you your entire life?

62344833
You won't make it as a blue collar wagie but that doesn't mean its a dead end

Open your own business and build, in the end youre the boss of blue collar wagies and make more than 100k, blue collar work is high in demand and always will, someone has to build the physical shit and it aont be KI

item.title: So the 2018 Farm Bill is expiring and the new farm bill is getting rid off all the hemp derived product flooding the mar… — 20 replies — 9.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
So the 2018 Farm Bill is expiring and the new farm bill is getting rid off all the hemp derived product flooding the mar…

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-10 09:50 UTC

OP

So the 2018 Farm Bill is expiring and the new farm bill is getting rid off all the hemp derived product flooding the market. Weed prices should be skyrocketing after November when the new laws take effect.

The problem is I don't know if this shit is already priced in and I don't know if I trust any of these scammy weed stonks. Still feels like one of those rare easy money opportunities to me. Especially with all the momentum for using psychedelics for PTSD and shit I can see them giving tons of weed to veterans to stop them from anheroing and stuff like this. Seems like zeitgeist is with weed right now. Im not from a legal state though so I wouldn't know how people there feel.

Early replies (first 3)

62343187
It is priced in and your intuition about weed stocks being a scam is 100% correct.

62343281
>>62343170
The goynip market is no where near as big as they thought it was going to be. Turns out most people don't want to smoke spiritual poison.

62343305
>>62343281
A big part of this is the fact that potheads have literally no standards. They will always buy the highest potency for the lowest price with zero regard for anything else. That ultimately means prices always drop until only a few major players remain that can handle the small margins. If those companies establish a monopoly and try to raise prices people just go back to the drug dealers that are literally too stupid to do anything but sell weed.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62343344
>>62343170
What do you mean, expiring? Bills expire, like eggs from the grocery store

62343419
>>62343281
That’s not true at all, it just doesn’t translate well to stocks. It’s like trying to get rich on egg stocks

62343430
>>62343170
weed costs about as much to grow as hay.
the markup on weed is incredible, but it's not the farmers making that money.

62343508
>>62343430
Hemp costs about the same to grow as hay. Pretty sure all the legal marijuanna is grown indoors and costs a lot relatively.

All the hemp shit is coming off the market.

62343516
>>62343344
Im not a lawyer bro we had the 2018 farm bill and we are about to have a new farm bill with different rules I think its fair to say the 2018 one expired

62343529
>>62343508
>Pretty sure all the legal marijuanna is grown indoors and costs a lot relatively.
I believe you are incorrect

62343629
marijuana and hemp are not the same, hemp derived thc is wrung from the foulest stalks and leaves you can imagine never smoking. marijuana that you would actually want to smoke is a completely different beast

62343775
>>62343170
>Weed prices should be skyrocketing after November
Based. That’s when I’ll be harvesting

62344233
>>62343305
it literally grows from the ground

62344258
I’ve grown weed many times, even worked in the industry and everyone makes good points but I’ll offer my thoughts as well.
Cannabis is a healing plant but it can be very addictive when used without taking tolerance breaks. Especially difficult to sleep when you stop.

The modern cannabis is bred to be high THC but that can be a short high when lacking other cannabinoids and terpenes. Growing your own is the best thing to do if you’re able to. Growing out doors is even better. Sun grown flower can sometimes lack the thc of indoor but it makes up for it with longer lasting effects and huge yields .
The short duration high, vapes and extracts are the parts of cannabis that become even more addictive.
Respect the plant and you can have a great relationship

62344541
>>62344258
The only thing cannabis heals you from is a life of honest work and a close relationship with God.

62344547
>>62343281
>goynip

That’s a new one. Just lmao

62344614
>>62344258
After a certain age you're not trying to be blasted, just mellow high, and outdoor is enough for that. The chimera shit they're making for distro is disturbing, nevertheless teenagers having access to basically dabs of shatter compared to the mid sour diesel and blue dream shit I had to smoke at their age.

62344635
>>62344541
We have cannabinoid receptors, we were meant to use weed as pain relief and sleep aid.
Let me guess, you drink but think weed is evil?

62344790
>>62344635
No retard your cannabinoid receptors are meant for your own endocrine cannabinoids to bind to, not an exogenous plant to overstimulate

62345036
>>62343170
>So the 2018 Farm Bill is expiring and the new farm bill is getting rid off all the hemp derived product flooding the market
thanks for the heads up, been meaning to stock up before my blueberry pancake runs out. just bought an ounce of pablo's revenge and another of lava cake. only $45/oz
>>62343344
>What do you mean, expiring? Bills expire, like eggs from the grocery store
like a sunset clause, legislation can expire. 2018 farm bill banned hemp with more than .5% or so delta 9 (thc). delta 8 and thca are the two main workarounds to technically be in compliance.
delta 8 is weird and should be avoided. they put CBD concentrate in a solvent or something and somehow that turns it into delta 8 and they spray it on cbd flower and sell at gas stations.
THCA is "real" weed that is just harvested a bit early to ensure the d9 stays below the farm bill threshold. most thc in normal weed is thca until it is heated/decarboxylated into d9 thc.
basically because the thc isn't heated yet it's not d9thc and therefore doesn't violate 2018 farm bill. this is a very generous interpretation of the farm bill, but i have been buying it on normal looking websites for a few years now with no issues

62345047
>>62344635
>grown ass men using the same argument as we did when we were young enough to shoot blanks in the late 90s

item.title: How hard is EE uni? — 32 replies — 5.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
How hard is EE uni?

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-10 06:30 UTC

OP

How hard is EE uni?

Early replies (first 3)

62339490
>>62339486
AI, it's over. Only get licensing for a profession that is protected by the highest levels of corruption.

62339492
very, if you are not comfortable with math and physics, or don't have an iq of at least 120, you will be overwhelmed by the workload and labs and assignments

62339496
>>62339492
Where do the 115-118 IQ engineers go when they realize they're not the next Nikola Tesla?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339517
>>62339496
maintenance work, going from site to site to diagnose faults, basically a tradie job, or you can join a epc firm and start designing electrical layouts in housing and substations, it really depends on your connections, the really high iq ones are starting their own companies or designing high end chips, i really don't see the point in getting into debt for this degree today with ai about to replace everything, unless you are doing it as a hobby, ai is already doing chip design

62339519
>>62339486
huge field, some are specialties harder than others; you'll make dog shit money for a few years until you get a handle on things and double or triple your salary.
Retards get auto-filtered and forced into finishing a business degree.

62339524
>>62339496
Some got leg lengthening and dental surgery and moved into higher management, but now, there is AI. Understand that.

62339542
>>62339517
Shouldn't EE's have good unions? I'm sure they can fight back against AI for a bit unlike programmers. Ofc they can't stop certain things such as AI making better chips but I'm sure they can retain some job security.

62339546
>>62339496
If they're still at university? Comp-sci. I'm not being a smartass. I was in a EE/comp-sci blended program. Had several classmates go, "I can't deal with the physics prereqs," and jump ship.

62339559
>>62339546
i don't even know why people compare comp sci with ee, comp sci is largely theoretical, most of the practical stuff you learn at work

62339563
>>62339542
ees won't be replaced like programmers, but i don't think new jobs will be created, they will just go to the current crop of engineers, most of the practical maintenance work will still be around, if you really want to hedge your bet, then do nursing or something related, that will likely be the last job that remains in demand

62339565
>>62339546
Huh I thought maybe they'd switch to mechanical engineering

62339569
>>62339486
It was very hard for me. Spent a lot of time having to study for complex math, circuit diagrams, fourier transforms, it was a fucking nightmare. Graduated at the tail end of the great recession, literally no one was hiring. Took a fucking glorified helpdesk job, worked my way up to DevOps because my degree was technically also CompEng, shifted to a different company focusing more on the FinOps side of things, and now I just do DevOps full time. Literally nothing to do with anything I spent 4 years of my life perfecting only to just throw it all away doing cloud work. Had an opportunity to start at micron a decade ago (talk about missed opportunities) but I probably wouldn't have gotten any stock options as a new grad so who knows.
Don't do this unless you want to. I enjoyed it, but what I got out of it more than anything was learning to work with Saudis and Chinks since those were my classmates, but don't even think about it being an instant golden ticket. Life's a journey so don't kick yourself if you have to work from the bottom up.

62339592
>>62339559
I think it's because they attract a lot of the same potential students: "I like computers and electronics." I can only speak for my experience, but the comp sci curriculum involved was mostly practical or foundational stuff: architecture, operating systems, networking, etc. Not the more esoteric math end of it. The mix with EE intended for targeting FPGAs, embedded, robotics, and automation/controls stuff. Basically, "know how to filter a ADC on either side."
Students would take intro to circuits and intro to programming the same year and decide they liked writing Hello World more than calculating Thevenin equivalents.

62339598
>>62339563
Oh boy are you deluded if you think you're some kind of special exception to the rule. You're already obsolete.

Muh Engineering.
Muh special skill.

Bye bye.

62339604
>>62339598
i am not saying they won't be replaced, i am saying it won't happen like programming since its a very wide field

62339621
>>62339592
i didn't know computer scientists do control, although its probably very introductory stuff, and fpgas? How do they even teach you this stuff without understanding circuits at a deeper level, or maybe they only teach how to program them and not how to design an fpga in a pcb,

62339677
Learn analog/digital hardware and some embedded programming, then try to get into industries where they require you to build highly specialized and custom embedded electronics, very lucrative but probably requires touching grass to even begin to know where the opportunities are. In electronics, you can pretty much teach yourself everything just get KiCad and read some Altium blogs to figure out what to do with traces and layer mangement, then send your gerber to some fab in China and you have a finished product. Still need real industry experience and good hardware skills to know what products are actually in demand. Stay away from copying the cool "projects" you see on Youtube, a lot of them are just performative LARPing and the concepts you learn from doing those projects might not be as applicable to what's actually needed in industry.


Never go into utilities or power engineering, the boomers stay there for 50+ years and refuse to to leave, even then they do they'll just hire some 24 year old son of some senior director to replace the boomer that just left.

62339686
>>62339677
Maybe that's still viable, but you need some moat against customers letting AI design & order the product for them. Risk & scale, for example.

62339741
>>62339686
If the AI is good enough build the product from scratch and order the proper components from the correct vendors then it's over. Doesn't matter how hard people cry about it, even if you beg for the government to ban AI to "SAVE MUH JOBS" it won't matter because the Chinese definitely won't give a shit and will go full AI adoption, and in the end the customers will end up ordering from them like they already do with majority of manufactured goods. I wouldn't worry about it until it happens. Just learn something else. If you can do it with EE, then you can do it with something else.

62339767
>>62339741
It can map very complex patterns in IT, and work autonomously for hours. That's because the data is good enough and public, IT cucks lack testosterone. Now AI corporations have access to the IP of every industry. If these API calls can be meaningfully used for training, they will find a way to force the customers to allow it, or just use it and get away with it because it's too profitable. I'd expect 12 to 48 months for end to end EE product development, depending on the political plays.

62339775
>>62339542
EE doesnt need unions. AI is only a threat to shit tier programmers. AI cant into embedded. Its hilarious to watch people try and use AI to make anything that connects to the physical world. It can make slop game, apps, and websites.

62339796
>>62339767
>the data is good enough and public, IT cucks lack testosterone.
the moat would be lucrative opportunities in fields that filter out the IT cucks where you have to service equipment outdoors where there's no ac and where the data is not good enough due to harsh operating conditions because average AI slop product would just get completely torn apart. Instrumentation for downhole drilling for example.

62339951
>>62339796
>harsh, torn apart
So we are back to fighting for a few jobs where the slaves are worked to death? Nice.

62342082
what am I gonna do.

62342136
>>62339542
AI is already used to make better chips. EEs don't give a fuck and just tell each other to git gud.

62342185
>>62339563
I'll just be making a little more than min wage as a nurse. Hours will be long too. I guess it's better than nothing.

62342217
>>62342082
https://ocw.mit.edu/
Teach yourself if you got the brains.
10 months ago I knew nothing. Now I can diagnosis pretty much any circuit AC/DC. I repair guitar amps, music equipment. Design circuits, make them.
look up the syllabus then search textbook_name.pdf. I use AI to make me questions to test myself.
There are lectures on youtube as well.

62342229
>>62342185
registered nurses make over 200k, it's a lucrative job and in extreme high demand

62342598
>>62339486
pretty easy and boring, should be harder desu; my iq is only 105 or so
however it's not optimal if you want to get chicks in college

62342635
>>62339496
industrial engineering or business and dad gets them a job

62344616
>>62339496
I work to live, not live to work. I want a Porsche and a house. Make enough to retire early.

62344643
>>62339496
Compsci, so you're still useful despite AI.
